platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock
details: **New open-weight models available (Feb 2026)**: DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5, Qwen3 Coder Next — frontier reasoning and agentic coding models. Source: https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/02/amazon-bedrock-adds-support-six-open-weights-models/
**Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.6** now listed as featured/available models on Bedrock. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
**Amazon Nova 2** now listed as a featured model. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
**Projects API support**: OpenAI-compatible Projects API now supported in Bedrock's Mantle inference engine. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/projects.html

**Server-side tools in Responses API (Jan 2026)**: Responses API now supports server-side tool execution via OpenAI-compatible endpoints, integrating with AgentCore Gateway tools — eliminates need for client-side orchestration. Source: https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/02/amazon-bedrock-server-side-tool-execution-agentcore-gateway/

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
details: **StopRuntimeSession API**: New API action for stopping a runtime session. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/APIReference/API_StopRuntimeSession.html
**Google ADK** added as a supported agent framework. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/using-any-agent-framework.html
**OpenAI Agents SDK** added as a supported agent framework. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/using-any-agent-framework.html
**Google Gemini** added as a supported model. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/using-any-agent-framework.html
**A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol** now supported. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-a2a.html

**Cedar policy language** explicitly supported in the Policy service as an alternative to natural language policies. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/policy-understanding-cedar.html

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **GPT-5.4 Pro and GPT-5.4 Thinking models** launched (Mar 25, 2026). Source: https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-4/
**Open Responses API** made available to the open-source community (Mar 25, 2026). Source: https://community.openai.com/t/open-responses-for-the-open-source-community/1371770
**Playwright MCP integration** added to Codex with approval workflow requirement (Mar 25, 2026). Source: https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp

**Computer action `keys` field**: New `keys` field added to Click, DoubleClick, Drag, Move, Scroll computer action types in Python SDK v2.30.0 and Node SDK v6.33.0. Source: https://github.com/openai/openai-python/releases

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **Lyria 3 Pro Music Generation — Public Preview (Mar 25, 2026)**: New music generation model capable of generating full 3-minute tracks with structural awareness (intros, verses, choruses, bridges), upgrading from Lyria 2. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/music/generate-music

platforms.name: Cursor
details: **Self-hosted Cloud Agents (Mar 25, 2026)**: Run Cursor cloud agents entirely within customer's own network — isolated VMs, full dev environments, multi-model harnesses, and marketplace plugins with full data residency. Source: https://cursor.com/blog/self-hosted-cloud-agents

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore A2A Protocol Support
findings.description: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore now supports the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol, enabling inter-agent communication across frameworks. Creates new entity types: A2A sessions, agent-to-agent message flows, and cross-framework agent connections. AISPM must discover and map A2A-enabled agents and their communication patterns to detect unauthorized inter-agent data flows

findings.title: OpenAI GPT-5.4 Pro and Thinking Models
findings.description: OpenAI launched GPT-5.4 Pro and GPT-5.4 Thinking — new frontier model variants with 1M token context, Tool Search capability, and enhanced computer use. AISPM must update model inventory to discover enterprise deployments using these models, track which agents and applications reference them, and ensure governance policies cover the expanded reasoning capabilities of the Thinking variant

findings.title: Bedrock Server-Side Tools in Responses API
findings.description: Amazon Bedrock's Responses API now supports server-side tool execution via AgentCore Gateway, eliminating client-side orchestration. This shifts tool execution from visible client-side calls to opaque server-side execution. AISPM must discover and inventory server-side tool configurations to ensure tool access permissions are tracked

findings.title: Bedrock Projects API (Mantle)
findings.description: OpenAI-compatible Projects API now supported in Bedrock's Mantle inference engine. Introduces new organizational entities (projects) that group models, deployments, and API keys with IAM-based access control and cost tagging. AISPM should discover project resources to map ownership and access boundaries

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore New Framework and Model Support
findings.description: AgentCore added Google ADK, OpenAI Agents SDK as supported frameworks and Google Gemini as a supported model. Expands the inventory of agent types that can run on AgentCore — AISPM must recognize agents built with these frameworks during discovery

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore Cedar Policy Language
findings.description: AgentCore Policy service now explicitly supports Cedar as a policy definition language alongside natural language. New entity type: Cedar policy documents attached to agents. AISPM should parse and inventory Cedar policies to assess agent permission boundaries

findings.title: Bedrock New Open-Weight Models
findings.description: Six new open-weight models added to Bedrock: DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5, Qwen3 Coder Next. AISPM model inventory must be updated to discover and track deployments using these models, especially for compliance with model governance policies

findings.title: OpenAI Open Responses API
findings.description: OpenAI's Responses API is now an open-source specification (backed by Hugging Face) enabling multi-provider interoperability. Enterprises may adopt this as a standard agentic interface across providers. AISPM should monitor for new Responses API-based agent deployments and cross-provider agent configurations

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore StopRuntimeSession API
findings.description: New `StopRuntimeSession` API action enables programmatic termination of agent runtime sessions. AIDR should monitor calls to this API as they may indicate incident response actions or unauthorized session terminations

findings.title: OpenAI Computer Use Keys Field Expansion
findings.description: OpenAI SDKs (Python v2.30.0, Node v6.33.0) added a `keys` field to all computer action types (Click, DoubleClick, Drag, Move, Scroll), enabling keyboard input during computer use actions. Expands the attack surface for computer-use agents — AIRT should test for keystroke injection and keyboard-based privilege escalation in computer-use scenarios

findings.title: OpenAI Playwright MCP in Codex
findings.description: OpenAI Codex now integrates Playwright MCP with approval workflows. Creates a new browser automation attack surface — AIRT should test for prompt injection via web content during Playwright-driven browsing sessions

findings.title: Cursor Self-Hosted Cloud Agents
findings.description: Cursor now supports running cloud agents entirely within customer networks with isolated VMs, full dev environments, and marketplace plugins. Deployed via Helm chart/Kubernetes operator with fleet management API. Introduces on-premise AI agent infrastructure that needs endpoint monitoring — code execution, tool access, and plugin activity occur within the enterprise perimeter

platforms.name: Azure AI Foundry
details: **Foundry IQ** — New service added to Azure AI Foundry documentation hub. Three new entries: "What is Foundry IQ?", "Frequently asked questions", and "Connect knowledge base to agents". Enables knowledge-base-connected AI agents. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/agents/concepts/what-is-foundry-iq

**Foundry Control Plane** — New top-level documentation section with entries for managing agents, models, and tools; evaluating agentic workflows; and monitoring generative AI applications. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/control-plane/overview
**Explore Foundry Models** — New concept card added to the documentation hub overview. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/

**Preview Inference API enhancements** — API reference now documents new preview inference API with monthly cadence, highlighting Structured Outputs (JSON schema via response_format), JSON Mode, and log probabilities parameters. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **Responses API with background mode** — New asynchronous file input processing capability for the Responses API (March 25, 2026). Source: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/background
**gpt-realtime-1.5 GA** — Community confirmation that gpt-realtime-1.5 is now generally available in the Realtime API (March 24, 2026). Source: https://community.openai.com
**ChatGPT Apps SDK** — New SDK actively developing with ui/notifications, tool-result handling, and model context update capabilities. Source: https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk

**MCP Apps Integration** — Model Context Protocol apps now integrating with ChatGPT with tool support and notification systems, expanded from previously seen "MCP Connectors". Source: https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/concepts/mcp-server

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock
details: **9 new foundation models** — Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6, DeepSeek V3.2, Kimi K2.5 (Moonshot AI), MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7 (Zhipu), GLM 4.7 Flash (Zhipu), Qwen3 Coder Next (Alibaba), Nova 2 (Amazon). Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-supported.html
**3 new model providers** — Moonshot AI, MiniMax, and Zhipu added as Bedrock providers. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-supported.html

**OpenAI-compatible Projects API** — New API support in the Mantle inference engine enabling OpenAI API compatibility. Projects provide application-level isolation for GenAI workloads. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/projects.html

**Server-side tools in Responses API** — New tool execution capability using OpenAI API-compatible service endpoints with AgentCore Gateway integration. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/gateway.html

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
details: **InvokeAgentRuntimeCommand API** — New API action for executing shell commands inside running AgentCore Runtime sessions with streaming output. Agents created after March 17, 2026 support this automatically. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-execute-command.html

**Google ADK and OpenAI Agents SDK** — Two new agent frameworks added to supported frameworks list. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html

**A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol** — New inter-agent communication protocol added to supported protocols. Enables agent discovery via Agent Cards and JSON-RPC communication. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-a2a-protocol-contract.html
**OpenInference protocol** — New observability protocol added to supported protocols. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/observability.html
**Google Gemini model provider** — New model provider added to AgentCore. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview** (Feb 19, 2026) — Advanced reasoning model with 1M token context window. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/models/gemini/3-1-pro
**GLM 4.7 GA** (Jan 20, 2026) — New model with complex reasoning and tool use capabilities, now generally available. Source: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes
**Gemini CLI Preview** (Jan 16, 2026) — Open source AI agent for terminal-based interactions with Gemini. Source: https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli

findings.title: Azure Foundry IQ
findings.description: New knowledge-base-connected agent service added to Azure AI Foundry. Introduces new inventory entities: Foundry IQ agents, knowledge base connections, and IQ query sessions. AISPM must discover and map these resources and their data flow to knowledge bases

findings.title: Azure Foundry Control Plane
findings.description: New centralized management layer for agents, models, and tools in Azure AI Foundry. Includes agentic workflow evaluation and GenAI application monitoring. Creates a new governance surface — AISPM should inventory Control Plane-managed resources and track agent-model-tool relationships

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock — 9 new models & 3 new providers
findings.description: Moonshot AI (Kimi K2.5), MiniMax (M2.1), and Zhipu (GLM 4.7/Flash) are entirely new providers. Plus Claude 4.6 variants, DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen3 Coder Next, and Nova 2. AISPM model inventory must be expanded to discover and classify these models and their deployments

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock OpenAI-compatible Projects API
findings.description: New API surface in Mantle inference engine provides OpenAI API compatibility. Creates a parallel invocation path that AISPM must discover — organizations may use this API to invoke Bedrock models without going through the native Bedrock API, bypassing existing monitoring

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore — A2A protocol & new frameworks
findings.description: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol enables inter-agent communication, creating new entity relationships. Google ADK and OpenAI Agents SDK are now supported frameworks. AISPM must map A2A connections between agents and discover agents built with these new frameworks

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore — InvokeAgentRuntimeCommand API
findings.description: New runtime API action for executing shell commands inside agent sessions. Extends the existing AgentCore API surface — AISPM should track calls to this endpoint for agent activity visibility

findings.title: Google Vertex AI — Gemini 3.1 Pro & GLM 4.7 GA
findings.description: New models available in Vertex AI Model Garden. AISPM model inventory for Vertex AI should include these models and track their deployments

findings.title: OpenAI Responses API background mode
findings.description: New asynchronous processing mode enables long-running file-input operations that execute in the background. AIDR must detect and monitor background response sessions — these async operations may process sensitive documents without real-time visibility

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock server-side tools in Responses API
findings.description: New tool execution capability via OpenAI-compatible endpoints with AgentCore Gateway. AIDR must monitor server-side tool invocations — tools executed server-side bypass client-side detection

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore OpenInference protocol
findings.description: New observability protocol for agent tracing. AIDR can leverage OpenInference traces as a detection data source for monitoring agent behavior

findings.title: OpenAI ChatGPT Apps SDK
findings.description: New SDK for building ChatGPT applications with UI components, notifications, tool-result handling, and model context updates. Creates a new app ecosystem — ENDPOINT must detect ChatGPT Apps running in enterprise environments and monitor their tool usage and data access

findings.title: OpenAI MCP Apps Integration
findings.description: Model Context Protocol apps now integrate directly into ChatGPT with full tool support. MCP apps can access local tools, files, and services — ENDPOINT must discover MCP app connections and monitor the tools they expose to ChatGPT

findings.title: Google Gemini CLI
findings.description: New open-source terminal AI agent for Gemini interactions. ENDPOINT should detect Gemini CLI installations and monitor terminal-based AI agent activity

findings.title: OpenAI MCP Apps — tool execution attack surface
findings.description: MCP apps connecting external tools to ChatGPT introduce prompt injection vectors through tool descriptions and results. AIRT should test MCP app tool chains for injection vulnerabilities and data exfiltration via tool responses

findings.title: Bedrock A2A protocol — inter-agent attack surface
findings.description: Agent-to-Agent communication creates new prompt injection vectors where a compromised agent could manipulate other agents via A2A messages. AIRT should test A2A chains for cross-agent prompt injection and privilege escalation

findings.title: Azure Foundry IQ — knowledge base poisoning surface
findings.description: Foundry IQ connects knowledge bases to agents, creating a data poisoning vector. AIRT should test whether malicious content in knowledge bases can manipulate Foundry IQ agent responses

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot (M365)
details: **Microsoft Agent 365 (early access preview)** added to the Copilot extensibility developer tools and APIs section. New agent platform entry for building M365-integrated agents. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365-copilot/extensibility/

Graph API: `administrationScopeTargets` relationship added to workflow base resources for targeting specific organizational units in lifecycle workflows (Feb 19, 2026). Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot Studio
details: **(Preview) Work IQ tools** — Connect Microsoft 365 Copilot and agents to the Work IQ service for real-time work insights from M365 files, emails, meetings, and chats (March 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new
**Claude Sonnet 4.5 (beta)** support added for Computer Use agents with improved nuanced decision-making (February 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

**Prompt builder enhancements** — Configurable content moderation sensitivity per prompt, new Claude model options (Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.5), inline prompt editing in agent tool details (February 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

**Improved agent responses** for ticket-based Microsoft 365 Graph connectors (ServiceNow, Azure DevOps) with better retrieval and actionable summaries (February 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

**(Preview) Expanded computer use capabilities** — New model support, built-in credentials, enhanced audit logging with session replay, Cloud PC pooling (January 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

**(Preview) Agent evaluation enhancements** — Thumbs-up/down feedback, activity maps for runtime visibility, CSV template for test sets (January 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

platforms.name: Azure AI Foundry
details: **Microsoft Foundry rebrand** — Azure AI Foundry documentation rebranded as "Microsoft Foundry" with new sub-services: **Foundry IQ** (knowledge base connections for agents), **Foundry Control Plane** (trustworthy AI, tracing, evaluation, monitoring), **Foundry Local** (run LLMs locally for free). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-studio/

**GPT-Realtime-1.5 and GPT-Audio-1.5** models released with improved instruction following, multi-lingual support, and tool calling for voice-first applications (February 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/whats-new

New preview inference API documented with v1 preview/GA versioning for data plane. Control Plane API updated to latest preview 2025-07-01-preview and GA 2025-06-01. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference

platforms.name: Power Apps
details: New **"Code apps"** option added under Create apps section. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/
New **"Overview of the new experience (preview)"** added under AI copilot capabilities (previously called "Vibe"). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock
details: **Claude Opus 4.6** and **Claude Sonnet 4.6** models now available on Amazon Bedrock (February 2026). Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html

**Six new open weight models** added: DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5 (Moonshot AI), Qwen3 Coder Next (Alibaba) — focused on frontier reasoning and agentic coding (February 2026). Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
**Three new model providers** on Bedrock: Moonshot AI, MiniMax, Zhipu. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
**OpenAI-compatible Projects API** support in the Mantle inference engine. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html

**Server-side tools support in Responses API** using OpenAI API-compatible service endpoints, with AgentCore Gateway tools integration enabling server-side tool execution without client-side orchestration (January 2026). Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
**Amazon Nova 2** model now featured. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html

platforms.name: Salesforce AgentForce
details: **Spring '26 Agentforce Voice** — New voice-first agent capabilities for customer experiences, including AI agent building for field representatives with voice command support. Source: https://www.salesforce.com/releases/
**Spring '26 Platform/Developer** — Enhanced DevOps capabilities, security improvements, Flow automation scaling enhancements. Source: https://www.salesforce.com/releases/

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **Partner Model Evaluations** (March 12, 2026) — Gen AI evaluation service now supports assessing partner models including Anthropic and Llama models through console-based workflows. Source: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes

platforms.name: Windsurf
details: **Cascade Hooks cloud configuration for enterprises** (v1.9552.21, February 12, 2026) — Enterprise governance feature allowing cloud-managed cascade hook policies. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
**MCP auto-triggered OAuth login** (v1.9566.9, February 25, 2026) — MCP refresh button and automatic OAuth login flow for MCP server connections. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
**POST_CASCADE_RESPONSE_WITH_TRANSCRIPT cascade hook** (v1.9566.9, February 25, 2026) — New hook type providing full cascade response transcripts. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog

platforms.name: Dia Browser
details: **v1.22.0** (March 11, 2026), **v1.21.0** (March 5, 2026), **v1.20.0** (February 26, 2026) — Three new releases. Feature details not available from release notes index page. Source: https://diabrowser.com/release-notes

findings.title: Microsoft Agent 365 (Early Access Preview)
findings.description: New agent platform entry added to M365 Copilot extensibility. Introduces a new agent entity type ("Microsoft Agent 365") in the M365 ecosystem that AISPM must discover and inventory alongside existing Copilot extensions, declarative agents, and API plugins. Microsoft Agent 365 is a new control plane for deploying, organizing, and governing agents at scale with integrated identity, compliance, and security from Entra and Defender. GA target: May 1, 2026

findings.title: Copilot Studio Work IQ Tools Integration
findings.description: New service connecting Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Studio agents to the Work IQ service for real-time work insights from M365 files, emails, meetings, and chats. Creates new data flow paths between agents and enterprise productivity data. AISPM must map agent-to-Work-IQ connections and AIDR must monitor data access patterns through this channel

findings.title: Microsoft Foundry Rebrand with New Services (Foundry IQ, Control Plane, Local)
findings.description: Azure AI Foundry rebranded as "Microsoft Foundry" with three new service categories: Foundry IQ (knowledge-base-connected agents, evolved from Azure AI Search), Foundry Control Plane (tracing, evaluation, monitoring), and Foundry Local (local LLM execution on Windows/macOS/Android). Introduces new inventory entities for each service. AISPM must update its Azure AI resource discovery to cover the new Foundry namespace and entity types

findings.title: Copilot Studio Computer Use Expansion
findings.description: Enhanced computer use capabilities with new model support (Claude Sonnet 4.5), built-in credentials management, Cloud PC pooling via Windows 365 for Agents, and enhanced audit logging with session replay. Expands the attack surface for agent-controlled desktop sessions — agents now have credential access and broader desktop control. AIRT should assess prompt injection risks in computer use scenarios and credential exposure vectors

findings.title: Copilot Studio Third-Party Model Support (Claude Sonnet 4.5, Opus 4.6)
findings.description: Copilot Studio prompt builder now supports Claude models (Sonnet 4.5, Opus 4.6) alongside Microsoft models. Agents can use non-Microsoft LLMs, creating new model provider entities to track and new data flow paths to external model providers. AISPM must inventory which agents use which model providers

findings.title: Copilot Studio Configurable Content Moderation Sensitivity
findings.description: Prompt builder now allows configuring content moderation sensitivity per individual prompt (Low/Moderate/High for hate, sexual, violence, self-harm categories). Creates risk of agents with weakened moderation settings. AIDR should detect and flag agents with lowered content moderation thresholds as a violation rule

findings.title: Azure OpenAI GPT-Realtime-1.5 / GPT-Audio-1.5 Models
findings.description: New voice/audio models (gpt-realtime-1.5-2026-02-23 and gpt-audio-1.5-2026-02-23) released on Azure OpenAI for real-time voice-first applications with tool calling support. New model entities for AISPM inventory; voice-enabled agents introduce new interaction channels that expand the monitoring surface

findings.title: Power Apps Code Apps and AI Experience Preview
findings.description: New "Code apps" development mode (now GA) added to Power Apps — enables code-first apps using React/Vue within Power Platform with enterprise governance. Plus a revamped AI copilot experience (preview) via vibe.powerapps.com. New app type entity for AISPM inventory — code apps may have different security characteristics than canvas/model-driven apps

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock Model and Provider Expansion
findings.description: Six new open-weight models (DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5, Qwen3 Coder Next) and three new providers (Moonshot AI, MiniMax, Zhipu) added to Bedrock, plus Amazon Nova 2. Powered by Project Mantle inference engine. Expands the model inventory AISPM must track and introduces providers from new geographies (China-origin models) with potential data sovereignty implications

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock Server-Side Tools via AgentCore Gateway
findings.description: Responses API now supports server-side tool execution through AgentCore Gateway integration without client-side orchestration. Customers specify an AgentCore Gateway ARN as a tool connector, and Bedrock auto-discovers tools, selects them during inference, and executes server-side — all within a single API call. AISPM must discover these tool configurations and AIDR must monitor server-side tool invocations as a new activity type

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock OpenAI-Compatible Projects API (Mantle)
findings.description: New OpenAI-compatible Projects API surface in the Mantle inference engine (announced Feb 26, 2026). Projects provide logical boundaries for isolating workloads with IAM-based access control and cost tagging. AISPM must discover project entities alongside existing Bedrock resource structures

findings.title: Salesforce Agentforce Voice (Spring '26)
findings.description: New voice-first agent capability delivering natural, human-like conversations aligned to company brand, with real-time CRM record updates, workflow triggering, and API calling — all with full auditability through Data 360. Natively connected to Salesforce with first-class support for Amazon Connect, Five9, Genesys, NiCE, and Vonage. AISPM must inventory voice-enabled Agentforce agents and their data access permissions

findings.title: Google Vertex AI Partner Model Evaluations
findings.description: Gen AI evaluation service now supports assessing third-party partner models (Anthropic, Llama) through console-based workflows via Model Garden. Extends the evaluation surface to non-Google models, which AISPM should track for model governance coverage

findings.title: Windsurf Enterprise Cascade Hooks and MCP OAuth
findings.description: Cloud-configured cascade hooks for enterprise governance (v1.9552.21), auto-triggered OAuth for MCP connections, and new POST_CASCADE_RESPONSE_WITH_TRANSCRIPT hook type (v1.9566.9). Enterprise admin whitelist controls restrict which MCP servers developers can install using regex pattern matching

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock
details: **Bedrock Mantle launched** (Dec 3, 2025): Full OpenAI-compatible inference engine with Responses API, Chat Completions API, asynchronous inference, stateful conversation management, and simplified tool use integration. Three new IAM policies: AmazonBedrockMantleFullAccess, AmazonBedrockMantleReadOnly, AmazonBedrockMantleInferenceAccess. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/bedrock-mantle.html

**Converse API batch inference** (Feb 27, 2026): Converse API format can now be used for batch inference input data, providing consistent request format across models. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/batch-inference-data.html

**Fine-tuning via OpenAI-compatible APIs** (Feb 17, 2026): Reinforcement fine-tuning for open-weight models through Files API, fine-tuning jobs APIs, and inference APIs. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/fine-tuning-openai-apis.html

**Reinforcement fine-tuning** (Dec 3, 2025): New capability to improve foundation model performance through feedback signals. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/reinforcement-fine-tuning.html

**Guardrails code security enhancements** (Nov 19, 2025): Standard tier enhanced with code content filters and prompt leakage detection for coding use cases. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails-code-domain.html

**Cross-account Guardrails sharing** (Nov 21, 2025): Guardrails can now be shared across accounts within an AWS organization. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails-resource-based-policies.html

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
details: **Evaluate API action** added to Data Plane operations — new API for agent evaluation not previously in the API reference. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/APIReference/API_Evaluate.html

platforms.name: Amazon Q
details: **Account subscription limit doubled** (Feb 18, 2026): Maximum increased from 10 to 20 accounts per AWS Region per organization. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/doc-history.html

**GitHub conversation-based feedback** (Dec 29, 2025): Feature development workflow now uses /q command for conversation-based feedback instead of traditional GitHub review process. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/github-feature-development.html

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **Agent Designer** (Dec 19, 2025): New low-code visual tool for building agents, available in preview. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/agent-builder/agent-designer
**Cloud API Registry** (Dec 18, 2025): New API management service available in preview. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/api-registry/docs/overview

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock Mantle inference engine
findings.description: Fully launched OpenAI-compatible inference engine providing Responses API, Chat Completions API, async inference, stateful conversation management, and simplified tool use. Introduces new IAM entities (MantleFullAccess, MantleReadOnly, MantleInferenceAccess) and a new API surface that must be discovered and mapped. Previously reported at surface level; full scope now documented

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock Converse API batch inference
findings.description: Converse API format now supported for batch inference input, creating a new bulk processing surface. AISPM must track batch inference jobs using Converse format alongside existing batch endpoints

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock fine-tuning via OpenAI-compatible APIs
findings.description: Open-weight models can now be fine-tuned through Files API, fine-tuning jobs APIs, and inference APIs using OpenAI-compatible endpoints. New fine-tuning surface distinct from native Bedrock fine-tuning that must be inventoried

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails code content filters and prompt leakage detection
findings.description: Guardrails Standard tier enhanced for coding use cases with code content filters and prompt leakage detection. AIDR must detect when guardrails lack code-specific protections on coding-focused deployments

findings.title: Google Vertex AI Agent Designer
findings.description: New low-code visual agent builder in preview. Creates a new entity type (visually designed agents) that may bypass traditional code-based agent discovery patterns. AISPM must discover agents created through this visual tool

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock reinforcement fine-tuning
findings.description: New capability to improve foundation model performance through feedback signals. Extends the fine-tuning surface with a new training methodology that AISPM must track

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock cross-account Guardrails sharing
findings.description: Guardrails can be shared across AWS accounts within an organization. AISPM must map guardrail sharing relationships and verify shared guardrails maintain proper configuration across account boundaries

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Evaluate API
findings.description: New Data Plane API action for agent evaluation. Extends AgentCore's API surface and introduces evaluation workflows as trackable entities

findings.title: Google Vertex AI Cloud API Registry
findings.description: New API management service in preview. Introduces a registry of APIs that agents can access, creating a new inventory surface for API-to-agent relationships

findings.title: Amazon Q Developer conversation-based GitHub workflow
findings.description: Feature development now uses /q command for conversation-based feedback instead of traditional GitHub review. Changes the interaction pattern AIDR must monitor for Q Developer GitHub integrations

findings.title: Copilot Studio Work IQ tools (Preview)
findings.description: Agents access real-time M365 files, emails, meetings, chats via Work IQ service — new inventory entities and data flow vectors

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock six new open-weight models
findings.description: DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5, Qwen3 Coder Next — inventory must map these model deployments

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock Server-Side Tools
findings.description: AgentCore Gateway tools execute server-side without client orchestration — activity monitoring loses client-side visibility

findings.title: Anthropic Memory tool multi-platform expansion
findings.description: Agents persist/recall info across sessions on Bedrock, Vertex AI, Azure AI — persistent data exposure vectors

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore new framework support
findings.description: Google ADK and OpenAI Agents SDK now supported alongside CrewAI, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Strands

findings.title: Cursor Composer 2
findings.description: Proprietary frontier-level coding model with token-based pricing — new model type in AI coding endpoint

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot (M365)
details: Microsoft Graph API Changelog: Bicep infrastructure-as-code support added for User, Application, servicePrincipal, OAuth2PermissionGrant, appRoleAssignment, Group resources. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

Lifecycle Workflows workflow creation limit raised from 50 to 100. New resources: attributeChangeTrigger, groupBasedSubjectSet, membershipChangeTrigger, insights. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/
Event Hubs change notifications will retire SAS authentication — migrate to Entra ID RBAC. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/
New agentSignIn resource with agentType enumeration for identity blueprint principals. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/
New selfServiceSignUp, signUpIdentity, signUpStatus resources for external ID tracking. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/
17 new protocol type members added (implicit flows, authorization code variants, client credentials, Kerberos, SSO, OBO). Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot Studio
details: March 2026: Work IQ tools (Preview) — connects Microsoft 365 Copilot and agents to Work IQ service for real-time insights from M365 files, emails, meetings, and chats. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

February 2026 expanded: Claude Opus 4.6 added as model option. Prompt builder now supports content moderation sensitivity controls (hate/fairness, sexual, violence, self-harm). Inline editing of prompt instructions in agent tool details view. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

platforms.name: Power Apps
details: Studio/Mobile version 3.26034 now in Preview US region. Version 3.26033 broadly deployed to Canada, India, South America, Japan, APAC, Australia, UK, Europe. Version 3.26032 now in US and GCC. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/released-versions/powerapps

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: OpenAI Community Forum: GPT-5.4-nano Priority Service Tier experiencing inconsistent latency. Source: https://community.openai.com
MCP connector refreshing tokens on every tool call without session persistence. Source: https://community.openai.com
Codex severe performance degradation with multiple files. Source: https://community.openai.com
Platform logs not being deleted after 30 days as expected. Source: https://community.openai.com
Fine-tuning operations stuck in "Waiting" status. Source: https://community.openai.com
ChatGPT Apps enabled but not visible in UI dropdown. Source: https://community.openai.com

platforms.name: Claude Enterprise (Anthropic)
details: API Docs: Models API now returns capabilities object per model for programmatic feature discovery. Source: https://platform.claude.com/docs/api/getting-started
Response headers documented: request-id and anthropic-organization-id returned in every response. Source: https://platform.claude.com/docs/api/getting-started
Files API expanded: GET /v1/files endpoint added. Skills API expanded: GET /v1/skills endpoint added. Source: https://platform.claude.com/docs/api/getting-started
Third-party platform size limits documented: Vertex AI 30 MB, Amazon Bedrock 20 MB. Source: https://platform.claude.com/docs/api/getting-started
Build with Claude Docs: Full feature availability classification documented (Beta, GA, Deprecated, Retired). Source: https://platform.claude.com/docs/build-with-claude/overview
Structured outputs availability now explicitly excludes Vertex AI. Source: https://platform.claude.com/docs/build-with-claude/overview

platforms.name: Amazon Q
details: Q Developer Doc History: Full detailed changelog now captured with 60+ entries. Notable: Context hooks deprecated, remote MCP servers with OAuth, Q CLI v1.16.0 features, enhanced context management. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/doc-history.html
Q Business Doc History: Full detailed changelog captured with precise dates for all entries. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qbusiness-ug/doc-history.html

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock
details: Six new open-weight models: DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5, Qwen3 Coder Next. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
OpenAI-Compatible Projects API now supported in Mantle inference engine. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html

Server-Side Tools Support — AgentCore Gateway tools integrate with Bedrock models via Responses API without client-side orchestration. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
Claude Opus 4 deprecation warning: migrate to Claude Opus 4.1 before May 31, 2026. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/doc-history.html

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
details: Google ADK and OpenAI Agents SDK now listed as supported frameworks (alongside CrewAI, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Strands Agents). Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html
Gateway integrations now list Salesforce, Zoom, JIRA, Slack as supported third-party tools. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html
Browser service now supports Playwright and BrowserUse integrations. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html
Policy service supports both natural language and Cedar (AWS open-source policy language). Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html

Evaluations service integrates with CloudWatch and requires OpenTelemetry/OpenInference instrumentation. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html
AgentCore Doc History: No new entries since December 2, 2025. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/doc-history.html

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: Claude 3.5 Haiku deprecation added — shutdown July 5, 2026. Source: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes
Gemini 3.1 Pro, 3.1 Flash Image, and Gemini 2.5 Flash with Live API Native Audio model releases. Source: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes
Agent Engine expansion: Code Execution, bidirectional streaming, Memory Bank features. Source: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes
Veo 3.1 video generation with extended aspect ratios, 4K upsampling, advanced editing. Source: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes
Evaluation service now supports Anthropic and Llama partner models. Source: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes
Workbench: JupyterLab 4+ support, managed credentials, Confidential Computing, Gemini CLI. Source: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes

platforms.name: Cursor
details: v2.6 (Mar 19, 2026): Automatic stale code cleanup after each turn. Dynamic instrumentation logging. Plan file content in autocomplete. Image galleries. Multiline Plan mode questions. Source: https://cursor.com/changelog
Enhanced agent workflows, improved PR/review anchoring, smoother tool-use flows, more reliable MCP setup. Source: https://cursor.com/changelog
Clearer team/enterprise access controls. Enhanced Slack integration. Improved long-running session reliability. Source: https://cursor.com/changelog
Bug fixes: plan editor web link fixes, branch/PR status refresh, diverged branch blocker, MCP dialog stability, Debug mode port conflict resolution. Source: https://cursor.com/changelog
Documentation expanded to 200+ entries covering CLI tools, language-specific setups, Figma integration, test generation. Source: https://docs.cursor.com

platforms.name: Windsurf
details: v1.9577.43 (Mar 19): Fixed Mac x64 build issues. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
v1.9577.42 (Mar 19): Stability and performance enhancements. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
GPT-5.4 Mini (Mar 17): Now available at 1x credits with promotional pricing. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
v1.9577.27 (Mar 12): Fixed Apple M5 processor compatibility. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
v1.9577.24 expanded details: Diff Zone fixes, AGENTS.md fix, improved SSH/Remote performance. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
Deprecated features: Windsurf Browser (deprecated Sep 2025), Knowledge Base removed, Legacy Mode removed. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog

platforms.name: Perplexity
details: Agent API now described with "model behavior controls". Embeddings API includes "retrieval workflow guides". Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/changelog
MCP Server tools enumerated: perplexity_search, perplexity_ask, perplexity_research, perplexity_reason. Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/changelog
API key rotation recommendation: every 30-90 days. Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/changelog
API Reference: /v1/agent endpoint confirmed (migration from /v1/chat/completions). Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/api-reference
Model fallback chains documented (up to 5 models). Presets: "fast-search", "pro-search", "deep-research". Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/api-reference
New parameters: instructions (custom system instructions), research_loop_steps (1-10). Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/api-reference

platforms.name: Dia Browser
details: v1.23.0 (Mar 19, Issue #17): GitHub Live Tab Groups — PR Status Celebrations animations, Automatic Cleanup on PR merge, Recently Completed PR Preview (hover to restore), enhanced navigation animations. Source: https://diabrowser.com/release-notes

v1.22.0 (Issue #16, Mar 11) expanded: Proactive Suggestions AI-curated, performance fixes (sluggishness/memory leaks), profile switching shortcuts (Ctrl+1,2,3), Command Bar routing to Chat and Google Search. Source: https://diabrowser.com/release-notes

Security page updated: Independent security firm assessment detailed (code reviews, architecture analysis, exploit testing). AI data retention policy clarified — never used for training. Prompt injection safeguard note: some attacks can cause unexpected style/tone shifts without compromising system security. Source: https://diabrowser.com/security

findings.title: Microsoft Graph agentSignIn resource
findings.description: New agentType enumeration enables tracking of agent identity principals — Zenity must update Activity monitoring to detect and classify agent-type sign-ins vs human sign-ins

findings.title: Copilot Studio Work IQ tools (Preview)
findings.description: Agents can now access real-time M365 files, emails, meetings, and chats — creates new data exfiltration vectors through agent-accessible work context. Data Lens must monitor Work IQ tool invocations

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock Server-Side Tools
findings.description: AgentCore Gateway tools execute server-side without client orchestration — Activity monitoring loses client-side visibility into tool execution. Must adapt detection to server-side tool call patterns

findings.title: Anthropic Memory tool multi-platform expansion
findings.description: Agents can now persist and recall information across sessions on Bedrock, Vertex AI, Azure AI — creates persistent data exposure vectors. AIDR Findings must detect sensitive data stored in agent memory

findings.title: OpenAI MCP connector token refresh issue
findings.description: MCP connector refreshing tokens on every tool call without session persistence — potential token leakage/replay attack surface

findings.title: Microsoft Graph 17 new protocol types
findings.description: Implicit flows, authorization code variants, client credentials, Kerberos, SSO, OBO — expand the attack surface for token-based attacks. Activity detection rules need updating

findings.title: Microsoft Graph selfServiceSignUp resources
findings.description: New signUpIdentity and signUpStatus resources for external ID tracking — potential for unauthorized external identity creation

findings.title: Microsoft Graph SAS auth deprecation
findings.description: Organizations must migrate Event Hubs change notification auth to Entra ID RBAC or lose notification delivery

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore Gateway enterprise integrations
findings.description: Salesforce, Zoom, JIRA, Slack integrations — agents can now interact with sensitive enterprise systems. Activity monitoring needs to cover these tool invocations

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore Browser service
findings.description: Playwright and BrowserUse integrations let agents browse autonomously — Data Lens must track websites and content accessed by browser-enabled agents

findings.title: Vertex AI Agent Engine expansion
findings.description: Code Execution, bidirectional streaming, and Memory Bank — agents gain persistent memory and code execution capabilities. Must monitor for data persistence and code injection risks

findings.title: OpenAI platform log retention issue
findings.description: Platform logs not deleted after 30 days as expected — data retention violation may affect compliance monitoring

findings.title: Perplexity Agent API migration
findings.description: /v1/agent endpoint with model behavior controls and research_loop_steps — new autonomous agent patterns to monitor

findings.title: Microsoft Graph eDiscovery Beta APIs
findings.description: Legal discovery workflows may expose sensitive data through new API surface

findings.title: OpenAI ChatGPT pinned chats incident
findings.description: Service disruption on Mar 21 — monitor for data accessibility issues

findings.title: Anthropic structured outputs Vertex AI exclusion
findings.description: Platform parity gap may affect detection consistency

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock six new open-weight models
findings.description: DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5, Qwen3 Coder Next — Inventory must map these new model deployments. Open-weight models may have different security profiles

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore new framework support
findings.description: Google ADK and OpenAI Agents SDK now supported — Inventory must discover agents built with these frameworks. Expands the agent ecosystem needing posture assessment

findings.title: Claude Opus 4 deprecation
findings.description: Migrate before May 31, 2026 — AISPM Violations should flag Opus 4 usage as deprecated model risk

findings.title: Vertex AI Claude 3.5 Haiku deprecation
findings.description: Shutdown July 5, 2026 — Inventory must identify all Claude 3.5 Haiku deployments and flag for migration

findings.title: Copilot Studio Claude Opus 4.6 model option
findings.description: Content moderation sensitivity controls added — Inventory must track new model availability. Violations should verify moderation controls are properly configured

findings.title: Microsoft Graph Lifecycle Workflows doubled
findings.description: Limit raised 50→100 — organizations can deploy more automated identity workflows. Inventory must track expanded automation

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock OpenAI-Compatible Projects API
findings.description: New API surface on Mantle for model access needs inventory mapping

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore Cedar policy language
findings.description: Organizations may define agent policies in Cedar — Violations should check for misconfigured or overly permissive agent policies

findings.title: Anthropic Models API capabilities object
findings.description: Enables programmatic model capability discovery — Inventory can leverage this for automated model capability mapping

findings.title: Anthropic Files and Skills API GET endpoints
findings.description: New API surfaces for listing files and skills — Inventory should map these resources. Violations should check for exposed/shared files

findings.title: Perplexity MCP Server tools
findings.description: perplexity_search, perplexity_ask, perplexity_research, perplexity_reason — new MCP tools to include in agent tool Inventory

findings.title: Microsoft Graph Schema Extensions GA in US Government
findings.description: Expanded government cloud capabilities may affect GovCloud compliance monitoring

findings.title: Vertex AI Gemini 3.1 Pro and Flash models
findings.description: New model releases require Inventory updates for organizations using Vertex AI

findings.title: Power Apps version rollout
findings.description: 3.26033/3.26034 deploying across regions — track version adoption for compliance

findings.title: Perplexity model fallback chains
findings.description: Up to 5 models in sequence — Inventory should capture fallback configurations

findings.title: Cursor v2.6 dynamic instrumentation logging
findings.description: Captures runtime behavior data from developer sessions. Enterprise access controls tightened. MCP setup changes may affect endpoint security posture

findings.title: Windsurf enterprise management features
findings.description: MDM config support, Devin service key authentication, org-wide command lists — new capabilities for endpoint inventory and policy enforcement

findings.title: Dia prompt injection safeguard acknowledgment
findings.description: Official docs state some attacks can cause unexpected style or tone shifts without compromising system security — confirms prompt injection as recognized limitation in AI browsers

findings.title: Cursor v2.6 automatic stale code cleanup
findings.description: Modifies code without explicit user action. Plan file content feeds into autocomplete — potential context leakage between sessions

findings.title: Windsurf GPT-5.4 Mini availability
findings.description: New model at 1x credits for AI coding — monitor for data handling differences

findings.title: Windsurf deprecated features
findings.description: Windsurf Browser removed (Sep 2025), Knowledge Base removed, Legacy Mode removed — endpoint capabilities changing

findings.title: Windsurf Apple M5 compatibility
findings.description: New hardware support expands endpoint surface

findings.title: Dia v1.23.0 GitHub Live Tab Groups
findings.description: AI browser automatically managing developer GitHub workflows with automatic PR cleanup

findings.title: Dia AI data retention policy
findings.description: Retained only for security/compliance, never for training. Independent security assessments scope detailed

findings.title: Perplexity MCP Server endpoint tools
findings.description: Four named tools (search, ask, research, reason) — endpoint-level MCP integration expands local AI tool surface

findings.title: Cursor documentation expansion
findings.description: 200+ entries including Figma integration, test generation, legacy code modernization

findings.title: Windsurf documentation update
findings.description: Onboarding process and incompatibility warnings with other AI extensions

findings.title: OpenAI Codex performance degradation
findings.description: Multiple files causing severe degradation — may affect developer workflow reliability

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot (M365)
details: Microsoft Graph API Changelog: New Bicep template support for user resource type (January 30, 2026), `activateWithScope` method for targeted workflow activation, `activationScope` resource with derived types. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

platforms.name: Azure AI Foundry
details: Azure AI Foundry Docs updated (Mar 19): New "Create apps with code" section with quickstarts, "Evaluate agentic workflows" and "Monitor your generative AI applications" added to Foundry Control Plane, Foundry IQ now includes "Connect knowledge base to agents", Microsoft Agent Framework now listed in resources. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-studio/

Azure OpenAI API Reference updated: Content Filtering now includes "jailbreak" and "protected material" categories, Parallel Tool Calls parameter documented (default: true), Managed Identity authentication options added, `max_completion_tokens` documented as replacement for `max_tokens`, Vision detail levels (low/high/auto) documented. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference

Azure OpenAI What's New: Minor enrichments to existing February 2026 and earlier entries (models available via Foundry, GPT-image-1.5 inpainting, transcription improvements). No new monthly sections. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/whats-new

platforms.name: Power Apps
details: New version releases: Studio/Mobile 3.26031 (first available Mar 2, worldwide Mar 23), 3.26024 (Feb 23 / Mar 16), 3.26022 (Feb 5 / Mar 3). Make.PowerApps.com 26032 (Mar 18 / Apr 1), 26023 (Feb 25 / Mar 11), 26015 (Feb 5 / Mar 2). Train 3.26023 cancelled due to Winter Olympics/Super Bowl/Lunar New Year. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/released-versions/powerapps
Power Apps Docs: New "Vibe" coding experience (preview) at /vibe/overview, "Create a code app" option, "Plan designer" feature added. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/

platforms.name: Salesforce AgentForce
details: Salesforce Releases page: Spring '26 now described as "AI-driven innovation across every cloud and industry." New webinar scheduled 3/3/26 for ISV partners. Expanded on-demand webinars: Agentforce Voice innovations, Marketing Cloud Spring '26, Field Service, Platform Roadmap. New product demo collections including Slack and Revenue Management as featured products. Source: https://www.salesforce.com/releases/

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: OpenAI Community Forum: gpt-realtime-1.5 is live in Realtime API (3,527 views); .NET libraries released for Agents SDK and ChatKit-style workflows; File upload problems with code interpreter in Assistants API; Signed URL file input logic broken; Codex consuming excessive credits during idle; Weekly usage limits exceeded without hitting 5-hour threshold; Codex data loss thread growing (25 replies, 986 views). Source: https://community.openai.com
OpenAI Status: Mar 13 — Responses API errors during background mode usage (resolved); Mar 13 — Conversation errors affecting logged-out users (resolved). Source: https://status.openai.com/history.rss
Python SDK (v2.29.0) and Node SDK (v6.32.0): No new releases since Mar 17. Source: https://github.com/openai/openai-python/releases

platforms.name: Claude Enterprise (Anthropic)
details: Anthropic News: No new announcements (latest: "What 81,000 people want from AI" from Mar 18). Source: https://www.anthropic.com/news
API Docs and Build with Claude: Fetch errors (Next.js rendering / 404). Source: https://platform.claude.com/docs/api/getting-started

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
details: API Reference: 3 new API operations added (total now 40, up from 37). New `Evaluate` operation for programmatic agent evaluation. `GetWorkloadAccessTokenForJWT` and `GetWorkloadAccessTokenForUserId` now explicitly listed. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/APIReference/API_Operations.html

platforms.name: ServiceNow
details: Fetch errors — both docs pages require JavaScript rendering. Manual review recommended. Source: https://www.servicenow.com/docs/r/release-notes/family-release-notes.html

platforms.name: Cursor
details: **Composer 2 released (March 19, 2026)**: New model achieving "frontier-level coding performance." Token-based pricing: Standard $0.50/M input, $2.50/M output; Fast (default) $1.50/M input, $7.50/M output. Source: https://cursor.com/changelog

platforms.name: Windsurf
details: **v1.9577.43 (March 19, 2026)**: Mac x64 build fix. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
**v1.9577.42 (March 19, 2026)**: Various bug fixes and performance improvements. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog

Earlier builds now show enriched details: SKILL.md loading from `.windsurf/skills/` directory, POST_CASCADE_RESPONSE_WITH_TRANSCRIPT cascade hook, windsurf.cascade.readClaudeCodeConfig flag, VS Code 1.108 merge, model pinning feature. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
Docs updated: System requirements section added, Custom App Icons (beta) for Mac, onboarding process with import from VS Code/Cursor. Source: https://docs.windsurf.com

details: API Reference expanded: Tools section now documents three categories (Web Search, Fetch URL, Function Calls). Streaming now specified as 14 distinct SSE event types with status indicators. Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/api-reference

details: **v1.23.0 (Issue #17, March 19, 2026)**: "From review to merge to what's next."
